titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 165
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

supportDesc.foliation: Modern foliation in pencil, 1-672, corrected in several places. In a few places, an earlier modern foliation, also corrected (e.g. and ).

collation.p: The manuscript is restricted from being collated, but the occurrence of catchwords at every 12 leaves throughout the manuscript indicates a structure primarily of senions, making 56 quires. Quire 51 () would in this case have had 14 leaves (lacking 1 between and ), and quire 56 11 leaves. That a leaf has been torn out between and also indicates that the current is an added singleton.

p.note: Top left corner, in ink by a modern hand: A 165. Bottom left corner, in pencil by a modern hand: 1695 no 10; below, in the same hand: 1698 no 1.
Bottom of page, in ink by a medieval or early modern hand: vxjx f.

binding.p: Late medieval or early modern binding. Brown leather over bevelled wooden boards. 10 double raised bands and endbands. 2 hooked clasps. Brass corner and centre pieces with bosses.

decoNote.p: , two concentric blind-tooled frames, the outer decorated with floral patterns and the inner with flowers alternating with diamond-shaped medallions containing an eagle with spread wings, the centre panel decorated with a pattern of small blind-stamped flowers and dots filled in with gold paint (some of the paint worn off). Inside the upper part of the inner frame, the title BIBLIA TEXT in ink. , three concentric blind-tooled frames, the outer decorated with blind-stamped foliage and diamond-shaped medallions containing a bird with spread wings, the middle panel with blind-stamped dots in sets of threes filled in with gold paint (some of the paint worn off), and the inner panel with floral patterns; the centre panel is decorated with two sets of floral shapes made up blind-stamped dots filled in with gold paint (some of the paint worn off) and a flower at the centre, also filled with gold paint. The corner and centre studs, as well as the catchplates and clasps are decorated with engraved patterns and embossments, and the edges of the studs are lined by a row of drilled holes which create a dentelle-like pattern.

binding.condition: The has been reinforced with modern leather. Straps of modern leather.

msItem.title: Account of the four senses of scripture
msItem.incipit: Sensus sacre scripture est quadruplex scilicet literalis et hystoricus figuralis et allegoricus moralis et tropologicus supernalis et anagogicus
msItem.explicit: Et isti tres ultimi sensus sunt parabolici

msItem.title: Prologue
msItem.rubric: Incipit epistula sancti ieronimi ad paulinum de omnibus diuine hystorie libris
msItem.incipit: FRater ambrosius tua michi munuscula perferens detulit

msItem.title: Genesis
msItem.incipit: Et factum est ita Viditque deus cuncta que fecerat
msItem.note: The beginning of the text is missing.

msItem.title: Prologue to Kings
msItem.incipit: Uiginti et duas litteras esse apud hebreos

msItem.title: Prologue to 2 Paralipomenon
msItem.incipit: EUsebius ieronymus dominioni et rogaciono suis

note.p: Designated here as the second book of Esdras.

msItem.title: Prologue to Tobias
msItem.incipit: CRomaclo et elyodoro episcopis ... Mirari non desino

msItem.title: Prologue to Esther
msItem.incipit: Librum hester uariis translationibus constat esse

msItem.title: Prologue to Job
msItem.incipit: COgor per singulos scripture diuine libros

msItem.title: Prologue to Ecclesiasticus
msItem.incipit: MUltorum nobis et magnorum per legem

msItem.title: Prologue to Jeremias
msItem.incipit: IHeremias propheta cui hic prologus scribitur

msItem.title: Prologue to Baruch
msItem.incipit: Liber iste qui baruch nomine prenotatur

msItem.title: Prologue to Ezechiel
msItem.incipit: EZechiel propheta cum ioachim rege iuda

msItem.title: Prologue to Osee
msItem.incipit: TEmporibus Ozie et Ioathe Achaz et Ezechie regum iuda

msItem.title: Prologue to Amos
msItem.incipit: OZias rex cum dei religione sollicite emularetur

msItem.title: Prologue to Micheas
msItem.incipit: TEmporibus Ioathe et Achaz et Ezechie regum Iuda

msItem.title: Prologue to Habacuc
msItem.incipit: QVatuor prophete in xii prophetarum uolumine sunt

msItem.title: Prologue to Machabees
msItem.incipit: DOmino excellentissimo et in cultu christiane religionis strenuissimo ludouico regi

msItem.title: Prologue to Machabees
msItem.incipit: REuerendissimo domino et omni caritatis officio dignissimo geroldo

msItem.title: Prologue to Matthew
msItem.incipit: MAtheus ex iudea sicut in ordine primus ponitur

msItem.title: Prologue to Luke
msItem.incipit: LUcas syrus nacione Antiochensis arte medicus

msItem.title: Prologue to the Catholic Epistles
msItem.incipit: NOn ita est ordo apud grecos

msItem.title: Interpretationes nominum hebraicorum
msItem.colophon: Deo gracias Finito libro sit laus et gloria christo Amen

supportDesc.foliation: Running titles and chapter numbers. : psalm numbers in Roman numerals (I-LXXXIX only) in red in the margin; guide letters visible ().
supportDesc.condition: Between and and and , respectively, one leaf has been torn out with loss of text in the first but not the second case. Top half of torn off. The initial on is (deliberately?) smudged.

p.note: Top left corner, in ink by an early modern hand: No 1. Below text, in ink by a medieval hand: Hec Biblia scripta est Anno Ihesu christi Rome Mlxii.
Top left corner, in ink by an early modern hand: N. 1..
In the margins in a contemporary hand, additions of missing lines with signes-de-renvoi.

decoDesc.p:
Main text in dark ink; rubrics in red; capitals touched in red. The decoration is identified by Roosval (1948) and Avril (1972)
as stemming from the Parisian school of Jean Pucelle; for a detailed discussion, see their respective op. cit.

decoNote.p: Each book of the Bible is introduced by a historiated initial over 6-9 lines with letter bodies in mauve or blue, often partially made up of grotesque heads combined with interlaced patterns, and with patterns in white and outlined in black; background in blue or mauve with geometric or foliate patterns in white and a square frame in gold paint or gold leaf outlined in black ink; counter space either in various chequered (e.g. ), diamond (e.g. ) or foliate patterns (e.g. ) in different combinations of blue, mauve, gold paint or gold leaf, with patterns in white, light blue, mauve, red, yellow, and sometimes gold paint, or with foliate patterns in a different shade of blue or red (e.g. ; one instance of black patterns on brown ). The major initials are accompanied by border decorations in the form of stems in blue, mauve, and gold paint or gold leaf, with highlights in white and foliage in red, blue, mauve, and gold paint or gold leaf; in the first part of the manuscript, drolleries, often in the form of hunting scenes (e.g. ), also appear in the border decoration but disappear after ; the border stems are occasionally also inhabited by dragons (e.g. , ). exhibits more or less all the features of the decoration scheme. The opening initial F, featuring St Jerome at his writing desk, extends the entire length of the text column and the accompanying border stem, with leaves, animals, and drolleries, fills the rest of the margin. The prologues are introduced by foliate initials over 4-6 lines in the same design and colour scheme as the historiated initials, but with the foliage on a background of gold leaf in the counter space; the initial for the prologue to John is more elaborate, with an eagle carrying a scroll with the name Iohanes in his beak (). The prologue initials also often extend into border stems in the margin. Chapters - or, for the Interpretationes nominum hebraicorum, new letters - are introduced by a foliate initial over 2-4 lines in blue or mauve with patterns in white on a background of gold leaf with 2 short stems (2 lines) extending upwards and downwards in the margin. Each verse of the Psalms () and each entry of the Interpretationes nominum hebraicorum () is introduced by a plain initial over 1 line alternating between blue with flourishing in red and gold leaf with flourishing in black. Many (but not all) rubrics have plain initials in blue with flourishing in red, or letter in gold leaf with flourishing in black (cf. Psalms etc.). Some rubrics have, instead, a pilcrow mark in the same colour scheme (e.g. ). The letters of the running titles and chapter numbers in alternate in the same colour scheme.
For further description of the decoration, see Eva Lindqvist Sandgren.
